framework.reasoning: Paper explicitly states PyTorch + PyTorch Geometric (TransformerConv) and that all reported training used CPU-only execution (device=cpu). No HuggingFace components are used. Plain YAML is sufficient given the modest, flat hyperparameter surface; Hydra/OmegaConf would add complexity not warranted by the paper's config needs.

module_hierarchy.description: Per-modality linear input adapters + modality-aware fusion (mean pooling weighted by observed-modality presence).

public_methods.name: forward
public_methods.signature: forward(self, modality_reps: list[torch.Tensor], presence_mask: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor

module_hierarchy.description: Spatial kNN graph builder + stacked TransformerConv encoder producing node embeddings Z.

public_methods.name: build
public_methods.returns: graph with edge_index [2,E] and optional edge_weight [E]

public_methods.name: forward
public_methods.signature: forward(self, x: torch.Tensor, edge_index: torch.Tensor, edge_weight: torch.Tensor | None = None) -> torch.Tensor

module_hierarchy.description: Feedforward reconstruction decoder mapping embeddings back to the full concatenated feature space.
Two-layer MLP projection heads used for the cross-modal NCE alignment loss.
Top-level LATTICE model wiring adapters -> fusion -> graph encoder -> decoder + projection heads.

public_methods.name: forward
public_methods.signature: forward(self, modality_blocks: list[torch.Tensor], presence_mask: torch.Tensor, edge_index: torch.Tensor, edge_weight: torch.Tensor | None, mask: torch.Tensor) -> dict[str, torch.Tensor]

public_methods.name: embed
public_methods.signature: embed(self, modality_blocks: list[torch.Tensor], presence_mask: torch.Tensor, edge_index: torch.Tensor, edge_weight: torch.Tensor | None) -> torch.Tensor

module_hierarchy.description: Spot-level multimodal dataset. Because the paper's private 11-sample melanoma cohort cannot be released (SIR ambiguity: data availability), this generates a SYNTHETIC dataset matching the described shapes (N spots, 5 modality blocks, D=5G) so the pipeline is fully runnable end-to-end.
Standalone kNN graph construction + optional Gaussian edge-kernel helper functions, reused by SpatialGraphBuilder.
Masked reconstruction (Huber, per Appendix H), cross-modal NCE alignment, and spatial smoothness losses; combined objective.
Training loop: builds graph, samples masks each epoch, computes combined loss, AdamW step, early stopping, checkpointing.

public_methods.name: save_checkpoint
public_methods.signature: save_checkpoint(self, path: str, epoch: int, is_best: bool = False) -> None

module_hierarchy.description: ARI, NMI, spatial contiguity, silhouette, and Multimodal Utility Score (MUS, Eq.11) computation for evaluation and Stage 6 comparison.
YAML config loading/validation and global seeding (Python, NumPy, PyTorch).

tensor_flows.name: LatticeModel.forward (training, masked reconstruction + alignment + spatial)
steps: for b in 1..5: adapted[b] = ModalityInputAdapter_b(modality_blocks[b]) -> [N,128]   # SIR: modality_input_adapters
x_tilde = (1-Omega) * X  (masking applied on raw concatenated X, decoder target)     # Eq.4
z = LatticeGraphEncoder(fused, edge_index, edge_weight) -> [N,128]                  # Sec 3.3, 3 TransformerConv layers, 4 heads
h_a = ModalityProjectionHead_a(z) -> [N,64]; h_b = ModalityProjectionHead_b(z) -> [N,64]  # Eq.7, pair=(Visium RNA, spatial ATAC)

tensor_flows.name: LatticeModel.embed (inference, no masking)
steps: adapted, fused as above (no masking applied)

tensor_flows.name: combined_training_objective

input.name: x, x_hat, mask, h_a, h_b, z, edge_index, edge_weight

steps: L_rec = masked_huber_reconstruction_loss(x, x_hat, mask, delta=1.0)   # Eq.6 (main text: MSE) vs Appendix H (Huber) -- ambiguity, Huber used per assumption
L_spatial = spatial_smoothness_loss(z, edge_index, edge_weight)       # Eq.9 / Laplacian form Appendix I.2

model.note_low_confidence: aligned_modality_pair ASSUMED from Appendix H (confidence 0.55, see SIR ambiguities)

training.beta_note: ASSUMED: PyTorch AdamW defaults, not stated in paper (SIR confidence 0.6)
training.lr_schedule_note: ASSUMED: no schedule described in paper (SIR confidence 0.3)
training.recon_loss_note: ASSUMED: Appendix H says Huber(delta=1.0); main-text Eq.6 says MSE -- internal paper inconsistency (SIR confidence 0.6)

data.edge_weight_mode_note: ASSUMED: unweighted kNN graph; Gaussian kernel sigma never specified in paper (SIR confidence 0.5)
data.data_source_note: SYNTHETIC DATA — real cohort is private/unreleasable (SIR ambiguity: data availability, confidence 0.8)

evaluation.leiden_resolution_note: ASSUMED: target K normally imported from companion SARSIM metadata, which is not public (SIR confidence 0.5)

runtime.reason: Core tensor/autograd framework; matches paper's PyTorch usage.
Provides TransformerConv graph layer used by the encoder.
Array ops, synthetic data generation, metric computation.
ARI, NMI, silhouette score, kNN utilities for evaluation.
Sparse graph utilities and distance computations.
Plotting for notebooks and evaluation reports.
Backend required by leidenalg for Leiden clustering.
Leiden community detection used for downstream spatial-domain clustering (Appendix H).

entrypoints.purpose: Train LATTICE on the (synthetic, by default) multimodal spatial dataset.

cli_args.help: Reduce dataset size/epochs for a quick smoke test

entrypoints.purpose: Compute ARI/NMI/spatial-contiguity/silhouette/MUS for a trained checkpoint across the modality ladder M1-M5.
Embed a single sample's spot-by-modality tensors into Z using a trained checkpoint.

risk_assessment.description: The real 11-sample, 54,912-spot melanoma cohort is private and cannot be released; there is no public dataset at this exact 5-modality Visium resolution.
risk_assessment.mitigation: Generated repo defaults to a synthetic dataset matching documented shapes (N=4992/sample, 5 blocks, D=5G, G in [129,322]) so the pipeline runs end-to-end; README clearly states that Table 2/3 numbers cannot be reproduced without the real cohort, and documents exactly how to plug in real data via data/README_data.md.

risk_assessment.description: Reconstruction loss form is inconsistent between main text (MSE, Eq.6) and Appendix H (Huber, delta=1.0).
risk_assessment.mitigation: Implemented Huber loss (Appendix H) as the default per the assumption, but losses.py exposes both `masked_mse_reconstruction_loss` and `masked_huber_reconstruction_loss` behind a config flag (`recon_loss: huber|mse`) so either can be selected.

risk_assessment.description: Cross-modal alignment pair scope is ambiguous (one stated pair vs. general formula for arbitrary pairs).
risk_assessment.mitigation: Config exposes `aligned_modality_pair: [0, 1]` (default, per Appendix H) but `nce_alignment_loss` in losses.py accepts an arbitrary list of pairs, so users can switch to all-pairs alignment by editing the config list.

risk_assessment.description: Whether spatial-loss edge weights use the optional Gaussian kernel (Eq.3, sigma unspecified) or a uniform/binary kNN adjacency is unconfirmed.
risk_assessment.mitigation: Default `edge_weight_mode: uniform`; graph_utils.py also implements `gaussian_kernel_weights(coords, edge_index, sigma)` behind the same config flag, with sigma defaulting to the median pairwise spatial distance if enabled.

risk_assessment.description: Missing-modality handling per spot (varying multiome cell counts, Table 1) is not explicitly specified.
risk_assessment.mitigation: ModalityAwareFusion accepts a per-spot, per-modality presence_mask and performs zero-imputation + presence-weighted mean pooling; documented as an assumption in the module docstring.

risk_assessment.description: Leiden target cluster count K normally sourced from the unpublished companion SARSIM paper's clustering metadata.
risk_assessment.mitigation: evaluation/metrics.py performs an independent Leiden resolution sweep over the configured resolution grid and selects K via silhouette score, rather than requiring the undisclosed SARSIM K.

risk_assessment.description: AdamW beta1/beta2 and LR schedule are not stated in the paper.
risk_assessment.mitigation: Config defaults to PyTorch AdamW defaults (0.9, 0.999) and a constant LR with early stopping, both clearly marked ASSUMED in config.yaml comments.

risk_assessment.description: leidenalg / python-igraph can be non-trivial to install in some CPU-only or restricted-network environments.
risk_assessment.mitigation: requirements.txt pins known-compatible versions; README documents a conda-based fallback install path via environment.yaml if pip wheels are unavailable.
